day1_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; tidal range is raising risk — large tidal swings resuspend bacteria stored in sediment and wet sand; and onshore wave energy is raising risk — onshore wave energy drives water — and any plume — toward the beach. The most recent sample, 7 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
day2_insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; and limited sunlight is raising risk (less UV to disinfect the water). The most recent sample, 6 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
